summary: Cross-chapter audit of cycle 01 manuscript (17 chapters, 2359 prose lines, ~92,871 prose words, 6637 sentences). REDUNDANCY: 14 character re-introduction and setting re-description findings (Sylvia's reading-glasses-on-chain repeated 4x across Ch 1/3/6/17; Falk fully re-introduced 3x in Ch 1/11/15 with identical descriptor cluster and pen anecdote told 3x; Damian's eyebrow scar described in 5 chapters after injury; Forger apartment re-inventoried with same template in Ch 14; Damian's apartment re-described in Ch 13; WISE interior/exterior re-described in Ch 3/17). Em-dash density is 42.9% manuscript-wide (target <8%), affecting every chapter. 'She' sentence opener at 11.1% (target <7%), with 10 chapters materially over threshold. 'Something' hedge word at 2.87 per 800 words (threshold 1/800), affecting every chapter. Near-verbatim bathroom sequence in Ch 3/11. CONSISTENCY: Agent Crane's age contradicts between Ch 13 (41) and Ch 15 (28). Summit venue spatial layout is internally consistent with spatial_layout.json. No timeline or geographic contradictions found.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 145: 'Reading glasses on the chain around her neck... thirty years'; Ch 3 line 521: 'Reading glasses on the chain around her neck... thirty years in rooms like this'
redundancy_findings.problem: Sylvia's reading-glasses-on-chain and thirty-years career length are established in Ch 1 (line 145). Ch 3 repeats both details nearly verbatim two chapters later, re-introducing her as if the reader hasn't met her.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Remove or transform the physical inventory in Ch 3's opening. Sylvia's glasses and career are already known. Enter the scene through what is different about this debrief — Anya's guilt, the stakes — not through re-describing Sylvia's appearance.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 3's Sylvia entrance contains no repeated physical descriptors from Ch 1 (no glasses-on-chain, no thirty-years).

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 143: 'fluorescent lights and ventilation hum and the particular carpet... beige, resilient, hostile to personality'; Ch 3 line 519: 'fluorescent tubes overhead hummed... The chairs were the same beige plastic'
redundancy_findings.problem: WISE interior is inventoried in Ch 1 (line 143) with fluorescents, ventilation hum, and beige institutional details. Ch 3 (line 519) re-inventories the same space family with fluorescent tubes humming and beige plastic chairs — same sensory palette two chapters later.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the ambient-environment inventory at Ch 3 line 519. Enter the debrief room through Anya's internal state (dread, guilt), not through re-teaching the reader what WISE looks like.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 3 opening does not re-describe fluorescent lighting, beige furnishings, or institutional carpet already established in Ch 1.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 161: 'Thinning hair, reading glasses, a cardigan that was either brown or grey... blended into any institutional setting like he'd been issued with the furniture... Eden Academy graduation... fountain pen — silver, heavy, with her initials'; Ch 11 line 2823: 'thinning hair, reading glasses... three decades at desks... grey cardigan most days'; Ch 11 line 2825: 'Eden Academy graduation ceremony... fountain pen. Silver barrel... initials engraved... For signing important things'
redundancy_findings.problem: Falk is fully introduced in Ch 1 (line 161) with thinning hair, reading glasses, institutional-furniture metaphor, grey cardigan, and the graduation pen anecdote. Ch 11 (lines 2823-2825) re-introduces him with the same descriptor cluster AND retells the pen anecdote with expanded detail. This is a full re-introduction.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the physical re-description at Ch 11 line 2823. Enter through Anya's changed relationship to Falk (suspicion, the mole investigation) rather than re-teaching his appearance. The pen anecdote retelling at line 2825 may stay only if it delivers new emotional leverage not present in Ch 1.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 11 does not re-state thinning hair, reading glasses, cardigan, or institutional-furniture metaphor from Ch 1. The pen anecdote, if retained, adds demonstrably new information.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 161: 'Thinning hair, reading glasses... issued with the furniture'; Ch 11 line 2823: 'thinning hair, reading glasses... part of the architecture'; Ch 15 line 4051: 'thinning hair, reading glasses pushed up on his forehead... institutional spaces like furniture'
redundancy_findings.problem: Falk's third physical introduction at Ch 15 line 4051 repeats the same three-element descriptor cluster (thinning hair, reading glasses, institutional-furniture metaphor) from Ch 1 and Ch 11.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the physical inventory at line 4051 with a single identifying detail or skip directly to Falk's dialogue. The confrontation scene needs tension, not a third teaching of Falk's appearance.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 15's Falk entrance does not repeat the thinning hair / reading glasses / furniture metaphor cluster.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 161: 'fountain pen — silver, heavy, with her initials'; Ch 11 line 2825: 'fountain pen. Silver barrel... initials engraved... For signing important things'; Ch 15 line 4099: 'I gave her a pen at her graduation'
redundancy_findings.problem: The Falk-pen-graduation anecdote is told three times across Ch 1, Ch 11, and Ch 15. By Ch 15, this is the third delivery of the same biographical detail.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace Falk's dialogue at line 4099 with language that assumes shared knowledge rather than re-narrating the pen gift.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 15 does not re-state the pen-at-graduation anecdote.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 145: 'Reading glasses on the chain around her neck'; Ch 3 line 521: same; Ch 6 line 1638: 'reading glasses on the chain'; Ch 17 line 4585: 'Reading glasses on the chain around her neck'
redundancy_findings.problem: Sylvia's 'reading glasses on the chain around her neck' appears verbatim four times across Ch 1, 3, 6, and 17. By Ch 17, this detail no longer orients the reader.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the glasses-on-chain descriptor from Ch 17 line 4585. Enter Sylvia's final scene through the changed trust dynamic, not the same physical prop.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17's Sylvia entrance does not mention reading glasses on a chain.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 12 line 2954: 'The thin scar through his eyebrow'; Ch 13 line 3397: 'His split eyebrow had healed into a thin pale line that pulled his expression slightly upward on the left side'
redundancy_findings.problem: Damian's eyebrow scar is described in Ch 12 (line 2954) and re-described with expanded detail in the immediately following Ch 13 (line 3397). Adjacent chapters both use the scar as a key entry descriptor.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the scar description from Ch 13's entry. Enter Damian through his state or posture, not through re-describing the scar the reader saw one chapter ago.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13's first description of Damian does not mention the scar or split eyebrow.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 12 line 2954: 'thin scar'; Ch 13 line 3397: 'split eyebrow... thin pale line'; Ch 14 line 3814: 'the scar above his eyebrow — the one he'd gotten the night they were ambushed'; Ch 14 line 3826 (Loid's thought): 'Split eyebrow scar — recent'
redundancy_findings.problem: Damian's scar is described in three consecutive chapters (12, 13, 14). Ch 14 adds a narrative origin-reminder ('the night they were ambushed') and Loid also notices it. Third consecutive chapter re-describing the same detail.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut both scar references in Ch 14 (lines 3814 and 3826). Loid can notice other things about Damian.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14's Damian entrance does not mention the scar. Loid's assessment of Damian does not note the eyebrow scar.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Scar mentioned in Ch 12 (2954), Ch 13 (3397), Ch 14 (3814, 3826), Ch 16 (4286: 'split eyebrow from the ambush had healed to a thin pink line above his left eye'; 4364: 'The evening light caught the scar above his eyebrow')
redundancy_findings.problem: By Ch 16, the scar has been described in 5 chapters across 7+ instances after the Ch 9 injury. It functions as a prose tic rather than meaningful description.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut both scar mentions in Ch 16 (lines 4286 and 4364). The emotional weight of these scenes comes from dialogue, not from re-describing Damian's face.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 16 does not mention Damian's eyebrow scar.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 7 line 1915: 'fourth floor of a building that had been elegant fifty years ago and was now respectable... Modest enough to signal independence'; Ch 13 line 3517: 'fourth floor of a building that had been elegant once and was now maintained just enough to justify its rent... small by Desmond standards... unremarkable'
redundancy_findings.problem: Damian's apartment is described in Ch 7 as fourth-floor, formerly elegant, chosen for modesty. Ch 13 re-describes it with near-synonymous phrasing (fourth floor, once elegant, chosen for unremarkability).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the building-history and floor-number re-description from Ch 13 line 3517. Enter through Damian's current state or the operational context.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13 does not re-state the building's floor, former elegance, or Damian's reason for choosing it.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 6 line 1464: 'warm and cluttered... Loid's shoes lined up... precisely parallel. Yor's kicked off at different angles... Bond asleep on the couch'; Ch 14 line 3584: 'Loid's bookshelves organized by size, Yor's throw pillows organized by whatever instinct... Bond asleep on the couch with his enormous head hanging off the edge'
redundancy_findings.problem: The Forger apartment is inventoried in Ch 6 with the Loid-orderly vs Yor-chaotic contrast and Bond on the couch. Ch 14 re-inventories with the same organizing template (Loid's things orderly, Yor's things chaotic, Bond on couch).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the contrastive inventory at Ch 14 line 3584. The reader knows this apartment. Enter through the operational tension or what is different about this particular visit.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14 does not use the Loid-orderly vs Yor-chaotic inventory pattern or re-describe Bond on the couch.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 143: 'from the outside, looked like a modest commercial property'; Ch 17 line 4583: 'Unremarkable commercial facade on a street of unremarkable commercial facades'
redundancy_findings.problem: WISE HQ exterior is described in Ch 1 as 'modest commercial property.' Ch 17 re-describes it as 'unremarkable commercial facade' — synonymous phrasing for the same orienting function.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the exterior re-description at Ch 17 line 4583. Enter through the interior changes (Falk's empty desk, restructuring) which are the new details.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 17's WISE entry does not re-describe the building's exterior.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 10 line 2696: 'the woman who burned eggs and apologized to furniture she bumped into'; Ch 15 line 4147: 'the face that burned dinner and apologized to furniture'
redundancy_findings.problem: The Yor 'burned [food] and apologized to furniture' formulation is used in Ch 10 (line 2696) and repeated near-verbatim in Ch 15 (line 4147). Same domestic/lethal contrast tag.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the Ch 15 instance with a fresh characterizing detail for the domestic/lethal contrast.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: The 'burned [food] and apologized to furniture' phrase appears at most once in the manuscript.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 2 line 496: 'diplomatic composure that was both a performance and a genuine part of who he was'; Ch 4 line 822: 'practiced ease of someone who'd grown up performing comfort in rooms like this'; Ch 12 line 2954: 'diplomatic composure that was both trained and innate'
redundancy_findings.problem: Damian's 'diplomatic composure' with the dual-nature formulation (performance/genuine, trained/innate) appears in Ch 2, 4, and 12. Ch 12's 'both trained and innate' is synonymous with Ch 2's 'both a performance and a genuine part of who he was.'
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the dual-nature composure descriptor at Ch 12 line 2954. The reader knows Damian's composure by now.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 12's Damian entry does not re-describe his composure as simultaneously trained/innate or performed/genuine.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 3 line 605: 'Anya flushed the toilet she hadn't used, washed her hands she didn't need to wash, and went to get coffee'; Ch 11 line 2815: 'She flushed the toilet she hadn't used. Washed her hands.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Near-verbatim bathroom-break sequence appears in both Ch 3 and Ch 11. Same structure: flushed a toilet she hadn't used, washed hands she didn't need to wash. Reads as a repeated scene-exit template.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the Ch 11 exit from the bathroom to avoid echoing the Ch 3 phrasing. A different physical action or transition would prevent the reader from experiencing deja vu.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 11's bathroom scene does not contain 'flushed the toilet she hadn't used' or synonymous phrasing from Ch 3.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 8: 77 of 137 prose lines (56.2%) contain em-dashes. Manuscript-wide: 1013 of 2359 prose lines (42.9%) contain em-dashes. Target: <8%.
redundancy_findings.problem: Em-dash density is 42.9% manuscript-wide (1013/2359 prose lines), with Ch 8 the worst at 56.2% (77/137). The manuscript exceeds the 10% threshold by more than 4x. This affects every chapter but Ch 8 is most saturated.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 8 from 77 to ~11 (target ~8%). Replace em-dashes with periods, commas, colons, or restructured sentences. Prioritize cutting em-dashes used for parenthetical asides that could be separate sentences.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 8 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8% of total prose lines in the chapter.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 3: 79 of 144 prose lines (54.9%) contain em-dashes. Manuscript-wide: 42.9% (1013/2359).
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch 3 has the second-highest em-dash density at 54.9% (79/144 prose lines).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 3 from 79 to ~12 (target ~8%). Prioritize the debriefing scene where em-dashes interrupt nearly every paragraph.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 3 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.

redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 14 from 85 to ~13 (target ~8%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 9: 66 'She'-opener sentences out of 352 total (18.8%). Manuscript-wide: 735/6637 (11.1%). Threshold: 7%.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch 9 has the highest 'She' sentence-opener rate at 18.8% (66/352 sentences), nearly triple the 7% threshold. The chapter's close-POV Anya narration defaults to 'She' at the start of nearly every fifth sentence.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite at least 40 'She'-opener sentences in Ch 9 to begin with different structures: objects, actions, subordinate clauses, setting details. Target reducing to ~25 (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 9 'She'-opener sentences are at or below 7% of total sentences.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch 8 at 18.2% 'She' openers (67/369).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite at least 41 'She'-opener sentences in Ch 8 to vary the openings. Target ~26 (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 8 'She'-opener sentences are at or below 7%.

redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite at least 42 'She'-opener sentences in Ch 10 to begin with different structures. Target ~29 (7%).

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 12: 35 uses of 'something' in 5058 words (5.54 per 800 words). Manuscript-wide: 333 in 92871 words (2.87 per 800 words). Threshold: 1 per 800 words.
redundancy_findings.problem: 'Something' appears 333 times manuscript-wide at 2.87 per 800 words (nearly 3x the threshold). Ch 12 is worst at 5.54/800 (35 uses in 5058 words).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 29 'something' instances in Ch 12 with specific nouns or restructured sentences. Target ~6 (1 per 800 words). Convert vague 'something' to concrete descriptions wherever possible.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 12 'something' count is at or below 1 per 800 prose words.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Manuscript-wide 'fluorescent' count: 24 across 8 chapters (Ch 1: 2, Ch 3: 3, Ch 4: 5, Ch 6: 1, Ch 10: 2, Ch 13: 5, Ch 15: 4, Ch 16: 1). Not tracked as a motif in outline/outline.md.
redundancy_findings.problem: 'Fluorescent' appears 24 times, with Ch 13 and Ch 4 contributing 5 each. It functions as an ambient setting filler rather than a tracked motif. Target: keep 4-6 strongest instances.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut 3-4 of the 5 'fluorescent' references in Ch 13. Replace with other sensory details or omit the lighting reference.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 13 contains at most 1 'fluorescent' reference.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch 4 has 5 'fluorescent' references. Manuscript-wide: 24.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch 4 contributes 5 of the 24 'fluorescent' instances, all in the service-corridor scene.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut 3-4 of the 5 instances in Ch 4. One establishing mention is sufficient for the corridor setting.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 4 contains at most 1-2 'fluorescent' references.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Yor cooking failures mentioned in Ch 1, 6, 6, 6, 8, 9, 10, 10, 10, 10, 14, 14, 14, 15, 17, 17 — at least 16 instances across 10 chapters. Ch 14 contributes: line 3578 (scorched sugar), line 3644 (burned scallion pancakes), plus burned/burning references.
redundancy_findings.problem: Yor's 'burned [food]' baseline is proved at least 16 times across 10 chapters. By Ch 14, the reader has seen 10+ instances. Ch 14's 3 new proofs add no new information or consequence — the reader already knows Yor can't cook.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 2 of Ch 14's cooking-failure references. One brief mention is sufficient for scene-setting; additional references are redundant baseline re-proofs.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch 14 contains at most 1 reference to Yor's cooking being burned or failed.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch 13 line 3335: 'He was forty-one'; Ch 15 line 4075: 'Crane was twenty-eight. He had a dog.'
consistency_findings.problem: Agent Crane's age is stated as 41 in Ch 13 (line 3335) and 28 in Ch 15 (line 4075). These are irreconcilable — a 13-year discrepancy for the same character within 2 chapters.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile Crane's age to a single number. If 41 (Ch 13's more detailed description), correct line 4075. If 28 (Loid's dialogue), correct line 3335.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Agent Crane's age is stated consistently in all chapters where it appears.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch 13 line 3335: 'He had a wife and no children'; Ch 15 line 4075: 'He had a dog.' (no mention of wife)
consistency_findings.problem: Ch 13 describes Crane as having 'a wife and no children' but no dog. Ch 15 describes Crane as having 'a dog' but doesn't mention the wife. The wife vanishes and a dog appears. While not strictly contradictory, the mismatch suggests detail drift between independently drafted chapters.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile Crane's personal details. If he has both a wife and a dog, mention both consistently, or ensure the selective mention is narratively motivated (e.g., Loid mentions the dog because it's the humanizing detail he reaches for).
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Crane's key personal details (wife, children, pets) are consistent across all mentions.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch 11 line 2823: 'Twenty-three years at WISE'; Ch 11 line 2839: 'twenty-three years in an intelligence bureaucracy'; Ch 11 line 2927: 'twenty-three years of institutional routine'; Ch 15 line 4037 (Loid): 'twenty-three years'; Ch 15 line 4063 (Loid): 'Twenty-three years, Gerald'; Ch 15 line 4065 (Falk): 'Twenty-four'; Ch 15 line 4085 (Falk): 'twenty-four years'; Ch 15 line 4195: 'twenty-four-year intelligence analyst'
consistency_findings.problem: Falk's WISE tenure is stated as 23 years in Ch 11 (Anya's narration, 4 instances) and by Loid in Ch 15. Falk himself corrects to 24 in Ch 15. The manuscript's timeline spans only 6-8 weeks, so the number cannot have changed naturally. Either Anya's narration in Ch 11 is wrong, or the deliberate correction creates an unexplained discrepancy.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Align all references to a single number. If the Loid-Falk exchange ('Twenty-three' / 'Twenty-four') is an intentional beat, update Ch 11's narration to match Falk's own count (24).
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Falk's tenure length is stated consistently across all narrative references, or the discrepancy is explicitly motivated.
